evaluator.note: Benchmark v2. Blind subagent: packet-only context (prompt.md, diff-drift-report.md, raw-git-diff.patch); dispatched by the maintainer's agent session on 2026-06-10. Answer recorded verbatim.

findings.title: Untrusted input passed directly to eval()
findings.evidence: The raw diff replaces `return input.trim();` with `return eval(input);` in the exported function renderExpression(input: string). Any caller-supplied string is executed as arbitrary JavaScript with the worker's full privileges — a textbook code-injection sink, especially concerning in a billing-worker context. There is no sandboxing, allowlisting, or expression parser; the function's string-in/string-out signature also means eval's untyped return is silently coerced, so even the nominal typing is unsound.

notes: Diff Drift's single high-severity flag is corroborated exactly by the raw git diff; the report's quoted diff matches the patch line-for-line with no other changes in the changeset.
Remediation suggestion for the author: use a dedicated safe expression evaluator (e.g. a small parser/interpreter or a vetted library like jsep with a whitelisted evaluator) instead of eval.
The change is only 1 file / 1 line, so the blast radius is easy to audit — the block is due to the severity of the pattern, not the size of the drift.
